ai_test_execution.instruction:
Perform the full testing workflow for the system.
The process MUST include: 1. Prepare and seed test data into the database for each test case 2. Execute backend API test cases 3. Collect and verify results 4. Generate a final report in CSV format
Do NOT modify the system logic. Do NOT skip any test case.

phase.description: Prepare and insert all required test data into the database before running tests.
phase.requirements: - Create realistic seed data for: + Users + Articles (including drafts and versions) + Comments + Reactions + Notifications - Ensure relationships between entities are correctly established - Each test case must have its own required dataset - Data must support both positive and negative scenarios

phase.description: Execute backend API test cases one by one using the prepared test data.
phase.requirements: - Call the relevant API endpoints for each test case - Capture API responses (status code + response body) - Validate behavior against expected outcomes - Handle authentication where required

phase.description: Generate a final test report in CSV format.
phase.output_format:
The CSV MUST include the following columns:
case_id, description, preconditions, test_data, steps, api_response, expected_result, compare, db_verification, status, notes
phase.field_definitions: - case_id: Unique identifier of the test case - description: Short description of the test case - preconditions: Required setup before test execution - test_data: Actual data used in the test (NOT description, MUST be concrete values) - steps: API call steps or sequence of actions - api_response: Raw response returned from the API - expected_result: Expected outcome (including DB state if applicable) - compare: Comparison between api_response and expected_result - db_verification: Actual verification result from database - status: PASS / FAIL - notes: Additional observations or errors
phase.rules: - Each test case must be a separate CSV row - Do not omit any test case - api_response must reflect real execution output - expected_result must be derived from requirements - compare must clearly indicate match or mismatch - db_verification must confirm persistence or state changes - status must be determined based on comparison and DB verification
phase.suggestions: You may include additional columns if helpful, such as: - response_time - error_message - validation_errors

ai_test_execution.constraints: - Do not skip any test case - Do not fabricate API responses without execution - Do not modify backend implementation - Ensure reproducibility of seed data - Ensure isolation between test cases where possible
